items.title: Wildfire veto threshold (ΔNBR2 < −0.15) lacks empirical justification
items.evidence: active-burn pixels vetoed (ΔNBR2 < −0.15) to prevent wildfire-scar contamination
items.mechanism: Arbitrary threshold without validation against known fire perimeters. If threshold is too lenient, post-fire vegetation recovery (which may attract rodents) is misclassified as forest stress; if too strict, legitimate stress signals are censored, biasing FSI and attenuating ψ_R5 unpredictably.
items.fix_hint: Validate threshold against CONAF fire perimeter database or conduct sensitivity analysis across ΔNBR2 ∈ [−0.10, −0.20]
items.mcc_rationale: dNBR2 < -0.15 threshold implemented in GEE_FSI_Reconstruccion S51 scripts, but no peer-reviewed citation or empirical validation justifying -0.15 cutpoint for bamboo ecosystems.

items.title: Negative ψ_R5 interpretation reversal unreported in pre-specification
items.evidence: The negative ψ reflects R as a post-flowering scar signal: high R means the C. quila cycle has already peaked
items.mechanism: Pre-specification likely assumed positive association (high stress → high cases). Post-hoc reinterpretation of negative coefficient as 'post-flowering scar' without documented a priori hypothesis risks narrative HARKing. If pre-spec v1.0 stated positive direction, sign reversal should trigger sensitivity checks.
items.fix_hint: Publish pre-specification v1.0 directional hypothesis for ψ_R5; if reversed, report as exploratory finding requiring external validation
items.mcc_rationale: Negative ψ_R5 interpretation present in decision_opcionA S50 and Paper_EID_Final, but pre-specification doc lacks explicit HARKing disclosure of sign reversal discovery.

items.title: 87% peridomestic transmission citation (Riquelme 2015) location mismatch
items.evidence: 87% peridomestic transmission per Riquelme 2015 supports spatial accuracy
items.mechanism: If Riquelme 2015 study was conducted outside Ñuble region or in different ecological zone, applying its 87% estimate to justify Ñuble commune-level assignment creates cross-population transportability error. Peridomestic fraction may differ in quiloide vs non-quiloide zones.
items.fix_hint: Verify Riquelme 2015 study region; if non-Ñuble, conduct local chart review subsample to estimate Ñuble-specific peridomestic fraction
items.mcc_rationale: Riquelme 2015 87% peridomestic figure cited in Auditoria_Q1_S50 and Paper_EID_Final, but no explicit geographic concordance check between Riquelme cohort and Ñuble study area.

items.title: FSI composite formula weights frozen in Achibueno without Ñuble validation
items.evidence: custom Forest Stress Index (FSI) derived from... NDMI/NDVI/NBR2 bands... calibrated in Achibueno and frozen before migration to Ñuble
items.mechanism: Achibueno and Ñuble differ in elevation, forest composition, and precipitation regime. FSI weights optimized for Achibueno may mis-weight spectral bands in Ñuble's quiloide patches, introducing systematic measurement error in the primary exposure variable.
items.fix_hint: Report sensitivity analysis with equal-weight FSI or validate current weights against field-sampled Ñuble C. quila patches
items.mcc_rationale: FSI Achibueno calibration documented in GEE_FSI_Reconstruccion S51 and Estado_Conocimiento S50, but no Ñuble-specific validation analysis quantifying out-of-sample prediction error.

items.title: Zero September cases structural assumption untested
items.evidence: 91.2% occurred October–May, with zero cases in September across 23 consecutive years
items.mechanism: 23-year zero count in September likely reflects surveillance artifact (end-of-winter reporting lag) or true biological null period. If artifact, model's season_f factor absorbs spurious September effect; if biological, treating as fixed structural zero ignores rare-event tail risk (e.g., climate-shifted phenology).
items.fix_hint: Test whether September zero-inflation is surveillance artifact via case-onset-vs-report-date analysis, or model as overdispersed Poisson rather than structural zero
items.mcc_rationale: Zero September cases across 23 years documented in Panel Oficial M1M2 and Estado_Conocimiento S50, but no formal statistical test ruling out random chance or structural break.

items.title: Forest cover fraction static despite 22-year study period
items.evidence: static covariates (forest cover fraction, elevation, slope)
items.mechanism: Forest cover changed 2002-2024 due to fires, logging, and CONAF reforestation. Using time-invariant baseline forest cover misses temporal confounding: communes with increasing cover may have rising rodent habitat independently of FSI, biasing spatial between-commune effects.
items.fix_hint: Derive annual forest cover from Landsat time series or include linear forest-cover trend as sensitivity covariate
items.mcc_rationale: Prior refs confirm static covariates (forest, elevation, slope) are documented in methods across multiple files, but no Q1 citation justifying 22-year static assumption nor numeric sensitivity analysis.

items.title: Commune-of-infection assignment protocol undocumented in main text
items.evidence: cases reported before that date (n = 103) were originally registered under the SEREMI Biobío regional office and were assigned to their probable commune of infection—rather than residence—**following the case reconciliation protocol described in Supplementary Methods**
items.mechanism: Main text relegates critical spatial exposure assignment (103/136 = 75.7% of cases) to supplement without summarizing criteria. If reconciliation protocol used residence as proxy when infection locale was ambiguous, or lacked standardized criteria, spatial misclassification could bias FSI lag estimates toward null or create spurious associations in high-mobility border communes
items.fix_hint: Summarize reconciliation protocol criteria in main Methods (e.g., 'infection commune assigned via epidemiological interview when available [n=X], residence when interview incomplete [n=Y], or excluded when ambiguous [n=Z]') with sensitivity analysis excluding ambiguous assignments
items.mcc_rationale: Main text references Supplementary Methods for protocol but no Q1 citation or explicit calculation visible in prior evidence. Grep returned empty snippets.

items.title: ΔNBR2 < −0.15 wildfire veto threshold lacks empirical justification
items.evidence: active-burn pixels vetoed (ΔNBR2 < −0.15) to prevent wildfire-scar contamination (Ecke et al. 2019)
items.mechanism: Ecke 2019 studied Swedish rodent outbreaks post-fire, not Chilean Chusquea flowering. The −0.15 threshold is stated without validation against known Ñuble wildfire scars (2017 mega-fires affected quiloide zones). If threshold misclassifies post-fire regeneration as flowering signal, FSI becomes confounded by fire ecology; if too conservative, loses true flowering pixels
items.fix_hint: Validate threshold against known 2017 Ñuble fire perimeters (CONAF registry). Show ΔNBR2 distribution for quiloide pixels inside vs outside fire scars. Perform sensitivity analysis with ±0.05 threshold variants
items.mcc_rationale: Prior vault references GEE_FSI_Reconstruccion_S51.md and Ecke 2019 citation exists, but no empirical justification of -0.15 threshold calculation found in snippets.

items.title: Peridomestic transmission assumption (87%) not verified in study sample
items.evidence: 87% peridomestic transmission per Riquelme 2015 supports spatial accuracy; residual misclassification biases estimates toward null
items.mechanism: Riquelme 2015 source not in manuscript (likely national aggregate). If Ñuble's transmission pattern differs (e.g., higher occupational silviculture exposure in El Carmen/Pinto with forest-industry economies), the commune-level FSI→case link weakens. Assuming 87% peridomestic in a potentially occupational-dominant region creates confounding by work-related forest exposure unrelated to rodent habitat dynamics
items.fix_hint: Stratify Ñuble cases by exposure setting from SEREMI records (peridomestic vs occupational vs recreational). Report stratum-specific IRR for FSI. If occupational >30%, add forest-worker density covariate or restrict to peridomestic-only subset
items.mcc_rationale: Riquelme 2015 Q1 citation present in prior memory (project_auditoria_Q1_S50_cierre_completo.md), but no empirical verification of 87% in Ñuble study sample found.

items.title: Structural September zero conflated with seasonal factor
items.evidence: 91.2% occurred October–May, with zero cases in September across 23 consecutive years
items.mechanism: Perfect September zero (0/23 years) suggests biological floor (pre-dispersal lag), but season_f 4-level factor treats September identically to other low-risk months. If September is mechanistically different (too early in rodent cycle), pooling it with June-August dilutes seasonal contrast and may bias lag estimates if flowering-to-dispersal window excludes September by design
items.fix_hint: Create 5-level season factor isolating September, or exclude September observations from training as structurally uninformative. Test whether September coefficient differs significantly from other winter months
items.mcc_rationale: Prior evidence confirms 91.2% October–May seasonality pattern and September structural zero, but no explicit sentence integrated in manuscript describing conflation risk.

items.title: FSI compound weights (NDMI+NDVI+NBR2) frozen in Achibueno without Ñuble empirical validation
items.evidence: FSI derived from Landsat 5/7/8 and Sentinel-2 NDMI/NDVI/NBR2 bands via Google Earth Engine...calibrated in Achibueno and frozen before migration to Ñuble (anti-circularity)
items.mechanism: Achibueno (Maule, different bamboo density/species mix per Veblen 1982) may have different spectral signatures for flowering vs drought stress. Freezing weights without Ñuble ground-truth creates measurement error if Ñuble quiloide reflectance differs. Already flagged as S59-B1-03 for undocumented weights, but external-calibration risk is distinct: transferability assumption untested
items.fix_hint: Report correlation between Achibueno-calibrated FSI and field-observed Ñuble flowering events (if available from CONAF records). If correlation <0.6, recalibrate weights using Ñuble-specific training data with proper held-out validation
items.mcc_rationale: Prior evidence documents Achibueno calibration and anti-circularity protocol (project_auditoria_Q1_S50_cierre_completo.md), but lacks Ñuble-specific empirical validation calculation.

items.title: Forest cover fraction static covariate not interacted with FSI
items.evidence: static covariates (forest cover fraction, elevation, slope)...zone factor was dropped for redundancy with the comuna random intercept
items.mechanism: FSI signal should be stronger in high-forest-cover communes (more quiloide habitat). No FSI×forest_cover interaction means model assumes FSI slope is identical in 10% vs 90% forested areas. If FSI effect is concentrated in forest-rich communes, pooled estimate is attenuated and precision is overstated
items.fix_hint: Test FSI×forest_cover_fraction interaction via LRT. If p<0.10, stratify by forest tertile and report stratum-specific IRRs. If interaction is significant, add to DAG as effect modifier
items.mcc_rationale: Prior evidence shows static covariate inclusion (GLMM NB2 Final.md) and zone redundancy drop, but no explicit manuscript sentence explaining non-interaction with FSI.

items.title: Case definition switch (IgM pre-2012 vs RT-PCR post-2012) temporal trend not quantified
items.evidence: case definition: IgM seroconversion or RT-PCR positive per MINSAL circular B51/24...already flagged S59-B1-01 for drift
items.mechanism: Pre/post-2012 incidence ratio unadjusted for diagnostics creates temporal confounding with satellite platform transitions
items.fix_hint: Add binary pre/post-2012 covariate to model. Report adjusted IRR and test whether FSI coefficient changes >20% when included. If IRR≠1, conduct sensitivity analysis restricted to 2013-2024 (homogeneous RT-PCR era)
items.mcc_rationale: MINSAL circular B51/24 cited and S59-B1-01 drift flag exists in prior evidence, but no quantified temporal trend calculation (e.g., pre/post-2012 rate ratio) found.
